metaBuilds.title: Bruiser Skirmisher
metaBuilds.whyLine: Strongest meta core for 10s rotation damage, online around minute 25 — about 420 damage over a 10s rotation in the sim.
metaBuilds.optimizer: Optimizer sees 3.4× the 10s rotation damage: try Spectral Schematics over Megacosm (sim-only — the popular pick may carry utility the sim can't see; test it).

matchups.gameplan: Your window is early: force trades before their core completes, and do not coin-flip fights past the crossover. Grux deals area damage — spread out so one cast can't catch the group.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Warden's Faith instead of Crescelia: their 3s all-in loses 31% of its bite; you give up 8% of your damage.

matchups.gameplan: They out-threaten you whenever the lane is not even: treat this as a farm-and-team lane, not a duel. Greystone deals area damage — spread out so one cast can't catch the group.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Warden's Faith instead of Crescelia: their 3s all-in loses 35% of its bite; you give up 8% of your damage.

matchups.gameplan: They out-threaten you whenever the lane is not even: treat this as a farm-and-team lane, not a duel. Steel deals area damage — spread out so one cast can't catch the group.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Void Helm instead of Crescelia: their 3s all-in loses 27% of its bite; you give up 8% of your damage.

matchups.gameplan: They out-threaten you whenever the lane is not even: treat this as a farm-and-team lane, not a duel. Bayle deals area damage — spread out so one cast can't catch the group.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Warden's Faith instead of Crescelia: their 3s all-in loses 32% of its bite; you give up 8% of your damage.

matchups.gameplan: They out-threaten you whenever the lane is not even: treat this as a farm-and-team lane, not a duel. Aurora deals area damage — spread out so one cast can't catch the group.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Void Helm instead of Crescelia: their 3s all-in loses 32% of its bite; you give up 8% of your damage.

matchups.gameplan: They out-threaten you whenever the lane is not even: treat this as a farm-and-team lane, not a duel. Zarus's main threat is Coliseum; bait it before you commit, and armor softens the trade.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Warden's Faith instead of Crescelia: their 3s all-in loses 32% of its bite; you give up 8% of your damage.

laneSteer.provenance: “Death From Above” ⇒ poke; the sim can’t model this augment — steered by the declared playstyle + field evidence — field offlane 49.6% over 110,669 games, magnitude not simulated.

metaBuilds.title: Bruiser Skirmisher
metaBuilds.whyLine: Strongest meta core for 10s rotation damage, online around minute 24 — about 420 damage over a 10s rotation in the sim.
metaBuilds.optimizer: Optimizer sees 3.4× the 10s rotation damage: try Spectral Schematics over Megacosm (sim-only — the popular pick may carry utility the sim can't see; test it).

matchups.gameplan: They out-threaten you whenever the lane is not even: treat this as a farm-and-team lane, not a duel. Khaimera's main threat is Cull; bait it before you commit, and armor softens the trade.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Warden's Faith instead of Crescelia: their 3s all-in loses 31% of its bite; you give up 8% of your damage.

matchups.gameplan: Your window is early: force trades before their core completes, and do not coin-flip fights past the crossover. Serath's main threat is Chastise; bait it before you commit, and armor softens the trade.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Warden's Faith instead of Crescelia: their 3s all-in loses 32% of its bite; you give up 8% of your damage.

matchups.gameplan: You hold the kill-threat edge wherever one exists: play front-foot, but respect jungle attention. Boris deals area damage — spread out so one cast can't catch the group.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Raiment of Renewal instead of Crescelia: their 3s all-in loses 21% of its bite; you give up 8% of your damage.

matchups.gameplan: You hold the kill-threat edge wherever one exists: play front-foot, but respect jungle attention. Yin's main threat is Backlash; bait it before you commit, and armor softens the trade.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Warden's Faith instead of Crescelia: their 3s all-in loses 32% of its bite; you give up 8% of your damage.

matchups.gameplan: They out-threaten you whenever the lane is not even: treat this as a farm-and-team lane, not a duel. Feng Mao deals area damage — spread out so one cast can't catch the group.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Warden's Faith instead of Crescelia: their 3s all-in loses 31% of its bite; you give up 8% of your damage.

matchups.gameplan: They out-threaten you whenever the lane is not even: treat this as a farm-and-team lane, not a duel. Kwang ramps on auto-attacks — pressure them before they itemize, and mixed resist blunts them later.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Void Helm instead of Crescelia: their 3s all-in loses 27% of its bite; you give up 8% of your damage.
